info.title: av_microservice_pas_cellc
info.description:
Integration services for accessing the Oracle PAS system in Cell-C\ \ Integration services for accessing the Oracle PAS system # *** Readme ***

Release Notes

VersionDetail
4.0.174Decommission hasTakenMax endpoint
4.0.173New hasTakenMax endpoint
This microservice acts as an intermediary between Oracle/ODS and the Airvantage microservices, in particular, the Profiler Database connectivity is done in the context.xml
All other settings can be found in the av_microservice_pas_cellc.setting table.

context.xml

Oracle Connection

Resource name="jdbc/oracleDB"

Profiler Connection

Resource name="jdbc/profilerDB"

Microservice connection - to access SETTINGS

name="jdbc/destinationDB"

av_microservice_pas_cellc.setting

  • LOGGING_MODE - DEBUG/ERROR
  • PROFILER_LOANABLE_AMOUNT_URL - URL for calling /getLoanableAmount in the Profiler.
  • AUTOLOAN_DENOM_LIST_ID - Which denomListID should be used for Auto-Loan options.

get.description:
This endpoint can be SYNCHRONOUS or ASYNCHRONOUS depending on the "sync" query parameter. Sends a request to PAS to create a loan. If used asynchronously, the response will only indicate that the request was received successfully. If used synchronously, the response includes the outcome after the request was processed. [Default serviceId=1] Oracle: {call p_confirm_advance(?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)}
With maxDenom, one call to addAdvance can initiate multiple advances. Example: if maxDenom=10, rechargeAmt=150, so fifteen advances of 10 each will be created.

parameters.name: repayDays
parameters.description:
How many days to repay this loan?
[Default = 30]

parameters.name: loanAmt
parameters.description:
Amount borrowed by subscriber OR
Amount being allocated to repaying the Loan (/addRepayment)

parameters.name: rechargeAmt
parameters.description: Amount being recharged

parameters.name: serviceAmt
parameters.description: Amount being allocated to the service fee

parameters.name: serviceId
parameters.description:
1 - Once Off - Airtime
2 - Auto - Airtime
3, 4 - Data
6 - MAX
[Default = 1]

parameters.name: channelId
maxDenom
parameters.description: Example: if maxDenom=10, rechargeAmt=150, so fifteen advances of 10 each will be created.

parameters.name: qualifiedAmt
parameters.description: The amount that the person qualified for at the time of granting the advance ("loanamount" field from getLoanableAmount)

parameters.name: sync
parameters.description: Is the addAdvance call synchronous or asynchronous? If 0 or omitted, then the response only indicates that the request was received successfully. If 1, then the request is handled synchronously and the response includes the result of processing the request

status.description: true if the request was received successfully (async) or if advance was granted successfully (sync)

comment.description: Contains a description of the failure if one occurred

parameters.name: transactionId
parameters.description: The Unique Transaction ID reference. This must be the same transaction ID that will occur in the clawback OCS ticket

parameters.name: clawbackId
parameters.description: The unique clawback transaction ID, as created by the EVM system

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: The clawback amount in cents

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: clawback date in the format yyyy/MM/dd HH:mm:ss

get.description:
Fetches Repaid loans from the CellC - PAS database, and writes it into the av_microservice_profiler.pr_loanrepayment table. Note:
Links for Oracle and MySQL in the context.xml

parameters.name: nbThreads
parameters.description: How many concurrent threads to spin up for this process. IMPORTANT: Each thread requires its own MySQL Connection.  So don't go crazy on this!  10-50 works well.

parameters.name: daysBack
parameters.description: Copy the recharges from ODS for N days ago

parameters.name: doAbort
parameters.description: If set to 1, the current thread performing the transfer will be terminated after the last batch update.

get.description:
Fetches Recharge Transactions from the CellC - ODS database, and writes it into the av_microservice_profiler.pr_transaction table. Note #1
Link for Oracle in the context.xml Note #2
Link to MySQL in the web.xml

parameters.name: nbThreads
parameters.description: How many concurrent threads to spin up for this process. IMPORTANT: Each thread requires its own MySQL Connection.  So don't go crazy on this!  10-50 works well.

parameters.name: daysBack
parameters.description: Copy the recharges from ODS for N days ago

parameters.name: doAbort
parameters.description: If set to 1, all the running threads for this endpoint will be terminated after completion of existing cycle.

get.description:
Returns a JSON response that indicates the current Advance status of a MSISDN. Response includes a statuscode
0 - Open loan, can't proceed
1 - Loan is presently being queued
2 - No outstanding loan
Oracle: {call p_advance_status(?,?,?,?,?,?,?)}

Multiple advances:
If there are more than one advance outstanding then this is what the response values mean:
* loanDate: The date that the oldest advance was granted
* loanAmt: The sum of the outstanding advances
* serviceAmt: The sum of all service fees for all outstanding advances
* loanBlance: Sum of outstanding amounts for all outstanding advances
* serviceBalance: Sum of outstanding service fee for all outstanding advances

Returns a JSON response that indicates the current Advance status for the given MSISDN of all supported service types.
1 - MoMo 2 - Airtime 3 - Data Response includes a entityTypeX

[ entityType1 / entityType2 / entityType3 ] 0 - Open loan
1 - Loan is presently being queued
2 - No outstanding loan
The response also includes totalrepaybalance

totalrepaybalance = totaladvancebalance + totalservicefeebalance

Overdue: If "overdue" = true in the response, it means that there is an unpaid open advance that is past the due date.

Queued: If "queued" = true in the response, it means that there is an advance that is in a queued state and currently being processed.

Note: This call gets cached if 'PROF_CACHE_LIVENESS_SECONDS'>0
In other words, calling the endpoint with the same query parameters within the 'PROF_CACHE_LIVENESS_SECONDS', will not access the DB again, but will simply return the previously generated http response.

Oracle: {call p_advance_status(?,?,?,?,?,?,?)}

Not available yet
Returns the last 20 records of a subscriber's advance history

Use this endpoint to retrieve the current value of the AutoTopup service fee.

Note that the value is cached, so run reloadSettings when there are changes
Compares ODS recharges to those loaded into the Profiler

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Date of comparison

parameters.name: asHTMLTable
parameters.description:
If set to 1, returns the comparison as an HTML table. 

Large differences are being highlighted.

response.value:
ODS Profiler
Count Sum Count Sum
1,568,567 20,254,933.02 1,348,716 20,254,933.02

get.description:
Returns the opt-in status (opAction) for various services.
1 - Opted In
0 - Opted Out Note: In this web service call, we're using the PAS-specific serviceId parameter instead of the pervasive entityType used in other calls. The primary reason for this is that PAS supports a few types not available in the rest of the stack (auto-loans, for example). Oracle: {call p_get_optin_status(?,?,?,?)}

parameters.name: serviceId
parameters.description:
1 - Airtime Once-Off
2 - Airtime Auto-Topup
3, 4 - Data

get.description:
Returns the subscriber's tenure (in months)

<font color=blue>Oracle</font>:<font color=maroon> {select floor(months_between(SYSDATE,max(fc_first_call))) from FC_FIRST_CALL where FC_MSISDN=?} </font>

Used to set/reset the opt-in status for various services.

<font color=blue>Oracle</font>:<font color=maroon> {call p_set_optin_status(?,?,?,?,?,?)} </font>

parameters.name: opAction
parameters.description:
1 - Opt IN
0 - Opt OUT

parameters.name: optInValue
parameters.description: Optional field to set additional values

parameters.name: serviceId
parameters.description:
1 - Airtime Once-off
2 - Airtime Auto-topup
3, 4 - Data

get.description:
Triggers the auto-loan process in the PAS system by
1. Checking if the person is opted into Auto-Loan: p_get_optin_status(?,?,?,?) 2. If the person is opted into this service, make sure the person does not already have an open AIRTIME loan: p_advance_status(?,?,?,?,?,?,?) 3. Check for an outstanding loan on DATA: p_advance_status(?,?,?,?,?,?,?) 4. Call the Profiler's /getLoanableAmount* with the value from step 3 as QueryParameterA to determine how much the person qualifies for
5. Calculate the advance amount- this is the maximum denom value <= the opted in value from step 1.
6. Add the advance to PAS: p_confirm_advance(?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?,?)
*
The microservice needs to know where to call the Profiler from, as well as which DenomListID and channelId should be used when profiling for Auto-Loans. This information is stored in the setting table:
AUTOLOAN_CHANNEL_ID
AUTOLOAN_DENOM_LIST_ID
PROFILER_LOANABLE_AMOUNT_URL

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description:
Timestamp of the event, comprising Date AND Time.
E.g. 2016/09/02 11:10:02

parameters.name: encode
parameters.description: encode =1 then the returned message will be URL encoded

get.description:
This endpoint is used to update the details of a requested advanced, for example to cancel or confirm an advance that was in a queued state.

values for return codes: 203 = successful advance
204 = business error
205 = http error
206 = request validation error

parameters.name: advanceId
parameters.description: The advance id from PAS (lra_uid) that needs to be updated

parameters.name: serviceQueueId
parameters.description: The service queue id (sq_uid in PAS) that was used in the original webservice call that PAS made

parameters.name: returnCode
returnDescription
parameters.description: The detailed description returned from EVM regarding the status of the recharge request

parameters.name: correlationId
parameters.description: The correlation id as returned by EVM
